questions.q: Who slew the demoness Tataka?
questions.explanation: Rama killed Tataka with his arrows, making it his first great deed of valor.

questions.q: At whose command did Rama attack Tataka?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishvamitra commanded Rama to slay Tataka so the hermitages could be free of her terror.

questions.q: What kind of being was Tataka?
questions.explanation: Tataka was a yakshini who became a fearsome rakshasi ravaging the forest.

questions.q: What weapon did Rama use to slay Tataka?
questions.explanation: Rama loosed his arrows against Tataka, ending her reign of terror.

questions.q: The slaying of Tataka is remembered as Rama's first what?
questions.explanation: Killing Tataka was Rama's first great deed of arms, marking the start of his career as a hero.

questions.q: Whom did the slaying of Tataka free from terror?
questions.explanation: Her death liberated the forest hermitages and the sages dwelling there from her depredations.

questions.q: What was Rama's stage of life when he subdued Tataka?
questions.explanation: Rama was still a young prince when Vishvamitra took him to the forest and he slew Tataka.

questions.q: Which brother accompanied Rama on the journey with Vishvamitra?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana went with Rama and Vishvamitra into the forest, sharing in the early adventures.

questions.q: Where did Tataka spread her terror?
options: In the forest and its woodlands
questions.explanation: Tataka ravaged the woodlands and forest, attacking the sages who lived there.

questions.q: Rama was a prince of which royal house?
questions.explanation: Rama belonged to the Ikshvaku line, the solar Raghu dynasty of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Why did Vishvamitra come to King Dasharatha's court before the Tataka episode?
options: To request Rama to protect his sacrifice from demons
To bring news of war with Lanka
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra came to Dasharatha asking for Rama to guard his sacrificial rites from rakshasa interference.

questions.q: Tataka had been transformed into a rakshasi by whose curse?
questions.explanation: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the sage Agastya cursed Tataka and her son, turning her into a man-eating demoness.

questions.q: Who was the father of Tataka?
questions.explanation: Tataka was born as the daughter of the yaksha Suketu, who had obtained her through penance.

questions.q: Which son of Tataka was also a demon associated with attacking sacrifices?
questions.explanation: Maricha was Tataka's son, and he later harassed Vishvamitra's sacrifice as well.

questions.q: Tataka was originally said to possess the strength of how many elephants?
questions.explanation: The Valmiki Ramayana describes Tataka as having the strength of a thousand elephants.

questions.q: What hesitation did Rama express before striking Tataka?
questions.explanation: Rama hesitated because of the moral scruple against slaying a woman, until Vishvamitra reassured him it was his duty.

questions.q: How did Vishvamitra resolve Rama's hesitation about killing a woman?
options: He said a king must destroy evildoers regardless of gender
He told Rama to spare her and bind her instead
He sent Lakshmana to do it
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra instructed Rama that protecting the world from a wicked being is a king's duty even if that being is female.

questions.q: The region devastated by Tataka was once a prosperous land belonging to which area?
questions.explanation: The Valmiki Ramayana says the flourishing Malada and Karusha lands had been laid waste by Tataka.

questions.q: How did Tataka try to fight Rama and Lakshmana during the battle?
options: By hurling rocks and creating a storm of dust
By summoning an army of demons
questions.explanation: Tataka attacked by raining down rocks and raising clouds of dust, using illusion and brute force against the princes.

questions.q: What did Lakshmana do to Tataka before Rama finished her?
options: Cut off her ears and nose / maimed her
questions.explanation: Lakshmana mutilated Tataka, cutting off her ears and nose, before Rama dealt the fatal blow.

questions.q: After Tataka's death, what did the gods and Vishvamitra do for Rama?
options: Praised him and Vishvamitra gave him celestial weapons
Crowned him king of the forest
Sent him back to Ayodhya at once
Cursed him for killing a woman
questions.explanation: Pleased with the deed, Vishvamitra bestowed divine astras (celestial weapons) upon Rama after Tataka's slaying.

questions.q: The Tataka episode occurs in which book (kanda) of the Valmiki Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The slaying of Tataka is narrated in the Bala Kanda, the first book describing Rama's boyhood deeds.

questions.q: By what name was Tataka's son Maricha's brother (another son of Tataka) known?
questions.explanation: Subahu was Tataka's other son; he and Maricha disrupted sacrifices, and Subahu was later slain by Rama.

questions.q: The forest haunted by Tataka lay near which river region in the tradition?
options: Between the Ganga and the Sarayu
On the banks of the Narmada
questions.explanation: Tataka's terrible wood lay in the region near the confluence of the Ganga and Sarayu, on the path Vishvamitra led the princes.

questions.q: What sacred lore did Vishvamitra teach Rama to dispel weariness on the journey before the Tataka fight?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra imparted the hymns Bala and Atibala to Rama and Lakshmana so they would feel no hunger, thirst, or fatigue.

questions.q: Whose husband did Agastya kill, leading to the curse that fully turned Tataka monstrous?
questions.explanation: Tataka's husband Sunda was destroyed by Agastya's power; when she and her son attacked the sage in revenge, he cursed them into demons.

questions.q: According to the Valmiki account, how did Rama finally kill Tataka after she rushed at him in fury?
options: He pierced her chest with an arrow
He beheaded her with a sword
He crushed her with a boulder
He burned her with a fire weapon
questions.explanation: Rama struck Tataka through the chest with his arrow, felling the demoness as she charged at him.

questions.q: What time of day did the battle with Tataka reach its climax, a moment Vishvamitra urged Rama to act before?
options: Twilight / nightfall, when rakshasas grow stronger
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra urged Rama to slay her before nightfall, because rakshasas become far more powerful and harder to defeat after dusk.

questions.q: The word 'yakshini,' describing Tataka's original nature, refers to a female of which class of beings?
options: Yakshas, nature spirits attendant on Kubera
questions.explanation: A yakshini is a female yaksha, a class of nature spirits associated with Kubera, the lord of wealth; Tataka was originally such a being.

questions.q: Tataka's father Suketu obtained her as a child through what means, having no offspring?
options: A boon from Shiva for valor
A gift from the ocean god
questions.explanation: The childless yaksha Suketu performed harsh penance, and Brahma granted him the daughter Tataka, blessed with immense strength.
